hero_subtitle: SFTY supports compliance teams by routing suspicious activity cases through audit-ready escalation workflows that link provider outputs, evidence, and regulatory rules for clearer decision-making.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling

trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Case escalation depends on manual routing
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to route suspicious activity cases efficiently when escalation workflows rely on manual handoffs and fragmented data. This creates delays, inconsistent decision-making and weak audit trails that complicate review and regulatory preparedness.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects alerts, provider outputs and case data into automated escalation workflows that guide suspicious activity cases through consistent review, decision logging and audit-ready evidence for MLRO teams.
workflow_title: From alerts to mlro case escalation
workflow_description: SFTY routes alerts through risk rules, review assignments and case creation to support structured escalation for MLRO review and audit-ready compliance evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond case chaos. Structured escalation control
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented AML alerts and provider outputs into managed escalation workflows, preserving evidence and decisions for clear audit trails and operational compliance control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASP compliance teams with structured case routing, preserving audit-ready evidence and supporting governance and deployment standards for bank and regulatory due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support AML case escalation workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how cases route into MLRO escalation workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence preserves suspicious activity reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how provider orchestration supports AML case management
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that connects risk signals, alerts, cases and decisions into structured workflows for AML teams in digital asset businesses like VASPs.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle case escalation workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes alerts into case workflows with assignment, escalation to MLROs, decision logging and evidence preservation to support operational control over AML case reviews and approvals.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready case evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, review history and supporting evidence in audit-ready packages to support defensible compliance reporting and regulator inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY connects outputs from KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one connected compliance workflow without disrupting existing integrations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace AML and risk providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY complements existing AML, screening and risk providers by structuring their outputs into workflows, cases, evidence and reports without replacing underlying systems.
final_title: Structure AML case escalation with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY routes suspicious activity cases into audit-ready MLRO workflows connecting evidence and regulatory rules.
